description: Project Brays: channel widening and retention basins. Only 40% of planned capacity delivered. Population in watershed grew 28% since 2010 while drainage capacity did not keep pace.
Built for 50,000 homes in 1940s, now serving 200,000+ homes. Sediment buildup has reduced effective capacity by ~15%.
Regional detention facilities insufficient for 100-year events. Northwest Houston development outpaced detention construction.
East Houston low-income area. Channel widening and deepening not completed. Frequent overbank flooding during heavy rainfall.
200+ stream gauges deployed, but AI-enhanced rainfall prediction still in pilot phase in 3 of 6 watersheds.
Impervious surface increased 25% from 1996-2017. No city program to offset new development with permeable alternatives.
